name: Tap-through
based_on: The mobile landing a printed QR code points to — the 'linktree-for-a-product' / 'scan-to-reorder' pattern used by coffee bags, restaurant table tents, packaging inserts, and event posters. Modeled here as a Portland coffee roaster's reorder page.
summary: A mobile-first landing that a printed QR code points to: a brand header, one featured product with a tap-to-copy promo code, a single primary call-to-action, a list of secondary links, and a QR code the page renders itself so visitors can re-share the link.
sections: Brand header with logo mark and city locator
'You scanned this' intro with scan-to framing and headline
One product / promo card with photo, tasting notes, price, and a tap-to-copy discount code
Secondary links list (brew guide, story, cafe, social, wholesale)
Self-rendered QR code pointing at the page's own URL, with a copy-link button
Footer with address, hours, and contact
setup_questions: What does the QR live on (packaging, poster, table tent, sticker) and what's the 'scan to…' framing?
What's the single action you most want the visitor to take?
What's the brand name, the one product or promo to feature, and the accent color?
Optional: 3–5 secondary links plus your address and contact.
